title: State ownership synchronization and recovery matrix closed
owner: LifeOS Data Agent / Runtime Kernel / QA Agent
objective: Reconcile the five parent authority contracts (PGAUTH-001 durable, PGAUTH-002 redb inventory, PGAUTH-003 cognitive, PGAUTH-004 projected, PGAUTH-005 external) and the seven concrete redb usage rows (PGREDB-001..007) into one complete authority matrix in which every recorded state family is assigned exactly once, every overlap and gap is resolved, every synchronization edge is directional and idempotent, every identity and recovery authority is named, no unresolved dual-write path remains, and every downstream execution target stays Draft while the global graph gate is closed.

recorded_state_family_reconciliation.coverage_rule: Each recorded family is covered exactly once; 'durable' splits into the relational and semantic owners STORE-001 names separately; the transient family's seven inventory rows are each resolved individually so no redb use is silently reclassified.

work_items.surface: Durable relational macro-state (stable identities, histories, permissions, provenance, module_path metadata, code AST/rows, causal dependency edges, blob references)
work_items.writers: PostgreSQL owns durable truth; envctl bridge performs the forward commit
work_items.idempotency: SHA-256 content-addressed identity makes re-commit of an identical blob a no-op (ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING)
work_items.identity_boundary: SHA-256 content hash (blobs) + stable PostgreSQL primary identities (PGAUTH-001 invariant 1)
work_items.forbidden_dual_write: redb is never a second durable writer for a PostgreSQL-owned class (STORE-001 invariant 1; PGAUTH-001 invariant 3)
work_items.relationship_to_postgres_redb_codedb: PostgreSQL canonical; redb transient forward buffer; CodeDB PgStore is the PostgreSQL-side BlobStore peer under one SHA-256 identity
work_items.decision: PostgreSQL + RuVector owns durable relational macro-state exactly once.
work_items.source: STORE-001 assignment 1; PGAUTH-001; pgauth_closure_matrix durable row

work_items.surface: Semantic / vector / knowledge-graph state (embeddings, hybrid-search indexes, RuVector GNN causal edges)
work_items.writers: PostgreSQL + RuVector; embeddings produced by local all-MiniLM-L6-v2 during the envctl bridge
work_items.sync_edge: text -> local embedding model -> committed to PostgreSQL, forward-only
work_items.idempotency: keyed by (content + model + version); deterministic re-embedding upserts to the same row (reproducible)
work_items.identity_boundary: derived-index identity bound to the source-row identity; never an independent authority
work_items.forbidden_dual_write: no separate vector-database sidecar; redb f32 distance math is cache-only (REDB-CLAIM-008/009/010 refuted, 013 qualified)
work_items.relationship_to_postgres_redb_codedb: RuVector lives inside PostgreSQL; redb only performs distance math on cached vectors; CodeDB feeds source rows the embeddings derive from
work_items.open_dependency: live RuVector-in-PostgreSQL extension activation not yet locally proven; full semantic cutover waits on it (STORE-001 open_dependencies; NBVERIFY-001 / REDB-CLAIM-023 remainder)
work_items.decision: PostgreSQL + RuVector owns the semantic family; indexes are reproducibly derived from versioned source rows and cannot become a separate authority.
work_items.source: STORE-001 assignment 2; blueprint 'No Sidecar / Trojan Horse'; PGAUTH-001 gate

work_items.surface: Transient high-frequency state (agent thoughts, terminal traces, intermediate logs, state flags, BLAKE3 dedup hashes, application-level WAL/outbox during PostgreSQL latency or outage)
work_items.writers: redb single writer per edge node / process
work_items.sync_edge: redb -> envctl flush -> PostgreSQL (only settled/significant states persist), forward-only
work_items.idempotency: content-identity dedup on flush; only significant states cross the boundary once
work_items.identity_boundary: agent-local / process-local keys (not a shared identity)
work_items.recovery_source: redb local disk (process lifecycle); on loss, state is transient by definition
work_items.forbidden_dual_write: redb never becomes durable authority for a PostgreSQL-owned class
work_items.relationship_to_postgres_redb_codedb: redb is the intended terminal transient tier; PostgreSQL receives only forward-flushed significant states; unrelated to CodeDB durable blobs
work_items.decision: redb owns the transient family as its intended terminal role; its seven concrete inventory rows are resolved individually in PGAUTH-006-R1..R7.
work_items.source: STORE-001 assignment 3; PGAUTH-002 inventory; blueprint redb scratchpad/WAL sections

work_items.surface: Per-agent cognition (MicroLoRA adapters, SONA policy matrices, RL weights, learning traces, witness-bound memory)
work_items.sync_edge: self-contained; one-way promotion of shared knowledge AgentDB -> PostgreSQL knowledge graph; no back-write
work_items.idempotency: SHAKE256 witness-chain identity makes promotion of the same fact idempotent
work_items.identity_boundary: agent identity + .rvf format; SHAKE256 Cryptographic Witness Chains rooted in PostgreSQL source vectors
work_items.recovery_source: AgentDB .rvf file; regenerable from base model + training history
work_items.forbidden_dual_write: AgentDB cannot silently own shared user-space truth (PGAUTH-003 invariant)
work_items.relationship_to_postgres_redb_codedb: AgentDB owns active cognition; provenance links back to the PostgreSQL knowledge graph; independent of redb transient tier and CodeDB blobs
work_items.decision: AgentDB owns per-agent cognition; it is non-authoritative over shared macro-state and promotes forward only.
work_items.source: STORE-001 assignment 4; PGAUTH-003; blueprint AgentDB / Witness-Chain sections

work_items.surface: Derived projections (materialized source directories for musl builds, exported release trees, generated files, build inputs, reports, disposable caches)
work_items.owner: PostgreSQL is source of truth; envctl / CodeDB materialization is the single deterministic read-only materializer
work_items.writers: envctl projection (read-only from PostgreSQL); single deterministic materializer
work_items.idempotency: deterministic byte-exact re-projection from the same branch/version yields identical SHA-256 output
work_items.identity_boundary: canonical source identity + selected branch/version + module_path metadata (PGAUTH-004 invariant 2)
work_items.forbidden_dual_write: projection output never becomes a competing writable owner and never back-writes canonical state (PGAUTH-004 invariants 1 and 2)
work_items.relationship_to_postgres_redb_codedb: PostgreSQL rows are the source; envctl reconstructs directories via module_path; CodeDB materialization is exact-byte from stored blobs
work_items.decision: Projections are disposable derivatives with one-way provenance from PostgreSQL.
work_items.source: STORE-001 assignment 5; PGAUTH-004; blueprint materialization/projection (four-step envctl)

work_items.surface: External source / secret authority (API keys, attestation bundles, source pins, externally authoritative services, devices, repositories, credential stores, human-approved records)
work_items.owner: OS keyring + committed source pins (external authority owns); LifeOS holds a reference
work_items.writers: keyring / external owner; governed inbound ingestion for external records
work_items.sync_edge: secret values: n/a (never captured); external records: inbound one-way ingestion with freshness/conflict policy + approval gate; outbound via governed handoff
work_items.idempotency: external identity reference; re-ingesting the same record updates in place (no duplicate authority)
work_items.identity_boundary: local reference key (never the secret value) (PGAUTH-005 invariants 1 and 2)
work_items.recovery_source: keyring + committed pins; fail-closed re-solicit / re-retrieve on outage
work_items.forbidden_dual_write: external service remains authority; LifeOS references only; no unresolved dual-write (PGAUTH-005 invariant 3)
work_items.relationship_to_postgres_redb_codedb: PostgreSQL stores references/provenance never raw secrets; redb never holds secrets
work_items.decision: External authorities own their state; LifeOS references, never captures, secret values.

work_items.surface: PGREDB-001 — CodeDB source_blobs via codedb_store_redb (content-addressed captured file bytes; durable DEFAULT store today; parity-tested by blobstore_parity.rs)
work_items.owner: Target canonical owner PostgreSQL via codedb_store_pg::PgStore under one BlobStore SHA-256 identity; redb role after cutover is transient forward buffer
work_items.overlap_resolved: Resolves the durable-today-vs-transient-target tension: the durable-on-redb default is TRANSITIONAL, not the target; it is NOT silently reclassified
work_items.sync_edge: capture -> store (selectable backend), forward-only; class leaves redb-durable only after a green live blobstore_parity.rs run against a disposable PostgreSQL
work_items.idempotency: SHA-256 content hash of file bytes, identical across backends
work_items.identity_boundary: SHA-256 content hash of captured file bytes (byte-exact, shared by redb and PgStore backends)
work_items.recovery_source: redb file + working tree today; PostgreSQL PITR after verified cutover
work_items.forbidden_dual_write: one SHA-256 identity across backends; migrate one verified swap at a time, never by assumption
work_items.decision: CodeDB durable redb blobs migrate to PostgreSQL-canonical under the existing SHA-256 parity contract; upgrades-not-downgrades honored by the migration MECHANISM.
work_items.source: STORE-001 correction + migration_path assignment 1; inventory PGREDB-001; ADOPT-001 (BlobStore, PgStore, SHA-256)

work_items.surface: PGREDB-002 — envctl migration_db (targets, runs, run_events, approvals, checkpoints, rollbacks; durable runtime store today, adopted via ADOPT-002)
work_items.resolved_family: PGAUTH-006-M3 (transient) for in-flight runtime; promotes to PGAUTH-006-M1 (durable-relational) for settled audit
work_items.owner: envctl redb is the transient runtime store for in-flight migration orchestration; settled/significant audit history promotes one-way to PostgreSQL durable audit tables
work_items.overlap_resolved: Separates the genuinely transient migration-run orchestration state from the durable audit history, so the durable use is preserved (promoted) rather than erased
work_items.sync_edge: envctl redb runtime -> one-way promote settled audit -> PostgreSQL, forward-only; audit class cuts over per-class only after verified parity
work_items.idempotency: envctl-internal run/event identity keys make promotion of a settled run idempotent
work_items.identity_boundary: envctl-internal migration/run/event identity keys (runtime); PostgreSQL audit-row identity for promoted history
work_items.recovery_source: redb file + envctl replayable migration engine (runtime); PostgreSQL PITR for promoted audit
work_items.forbidden_dual_write: envctl is not the synchronizing durable writer of macro-history; no undirected redb<->PostgreSQL dual-write (AUTH-CLAIM-001 refuted)
work_items.decision: envctl durable redb migration store is decided as transient runtime + forward-promoted audit; preserved, not erased.
work_items.source: inventory PGREDB-002 (REDB-CLAIM-020 qualified); ADOPT-002; STORE-001 transient class + verified-cutover invariant

work_items.surface: PGREDB-003 — high-frequency agent scratchpad (per-agent thoughts/state changes logged without network lag; proposed mechanism)
work_items.owner: owning agent runtime (single writer per scratchpad); redb intended terminal transient role
work_items.overlap_resolved: No conflict; assigned to redb as designed
work_items.sync_edge: one-way scratchpad -> optional curated flush of settled summaries to PostgreSQL macro-state; never authoritative
work_items.idempotency: disposable; a re-flush of the same settled summary dedups by content identity
work_items.recovery_source: none required (disposable); recomputed from agent context; safe to discard on crash
work_items.decision: Agent scratchpad is transient redb; only curated settled summaries flush forward.
work_items.source: STORE-001 assignment 3; inventory PGREDB-003; blueprint local-AI-scratchpad section

work_items.surface: PGREDB-004 — embedding cache (model-output cache keyed by BLAKE3 content hash to avoid duplicate inference; proposed)
work_items.owner: embedding pipeline (single writer); disposable dedup cache
work_items.overlap_resolved: No conflict; a cache miss is always safe (recompute); never authority
work_items.sync_edge: one-way canonical input -> cache entry; read-only on hit
work_items.idempotency: BLAKE3(content + model + version + config) key; a hit is read-idempotent, a miss recomputes deterministically
work_items.identity_boundary: BLAKE3 hash of (content + model + version + config)
work_items.recovery_source: recompute from source input + model on miss; safe to discard on crash
work_items.forbidden_dual_write: derived from canonical semantic rows; never a competing authority
work_items.decision: Embedding cache is a disposable transient redb derivative of the PostgreSQL semantic family.
work_items.source: STORE-001 assignment 3 (dedup cache) + assignment 2 (semantic canonical); inventory PGREDB-004; blueprint BLAKE3 dedup cache

work_items.surface: PGREDB-005 — application-level WAL / outbox (redb buffers outgoing PostgreSQL operations during PG latency/outage, retaining until connectivity returns; proposed)
work_items.resolved_family: PGAUTH-006-M3 (transient), subordinate to PGAUTH-006-M1 (PostgreSQL destination authority)
work_items.owner: outbox worker (single writer); durable-until-acked buffer subordinate to PostgreSQL
work_items.overlap_resolved: Distinguished from PostgreSQL WAL and from authority: it is a subordinate buffer, not a second durable owner
work_items.sync_edge: one-way local op -> outbox -> replay to PostgreSQL with ack; retry + dedup on reconnect
work_items.idempotency: monotonic sequence key + operation idempotency key give at-most-once apply on replay
work_items.identity_boundary: monotonic sequence key + operation idempotency key
work_items.forbidden_dual_write: PostgreSQL remains canonical; envctl is not the synchronizing durable writer (AUTH-CLAIM-001 refuted)
work_items.decision: Outbox is a transient, durable-until-acked redb buffer subordinate to PostgreSQL; never authority.
work_items.source: STORE-001 assignment 3 ('WAL during PostgreSQL latency/outage'); inventory PGREDB-005; blueprint resilient-WAL section

work_items.surface: PGREDB-006 — intermediate ingestion buffer (NotebookLM 'migration bridge' framing: parser -> redb buffer -> PostgreSQL). CONTESTED: buffer-only framing contradicted the durable-default reality and the fact that nu_plugin does not buffer parsed blocks into redb
work_items.resolved_family: PGAUTH-006-M3 (transient) as the forward buffer; durable authority is PGAUTH-006-M1 (PostgreSQL)
work_items.owner: redb is the transient forward buffer; PostgreSQL is the durable authority
work_items.overlap_resolved: THE central STORE-001 question, resolved: the contradiction between the source's transient-buffer framing and CodeDB's verified durable-default is settled by the PGREDB-001 (R1) verified cutover — redb becomes the intended transient buffer only as the durable class migrates to PostgreSQL under SHA-256 parity, so no durable use is silently reclassified
work_items.sync_edge: target: capture/parse -> redb buffer -> envctl bridge -> PostgreSQL, forward-only; current reality preserved until R1 cutover verifies
work_items.idempotency: SHA-256 blob identity shared with PGREDB-001
work_items.recovery_source: redb file today; PostgreSQL after cutover
work_items.forbidden_dual_write: single durable owner (PostgreSQL) after verified cutover; redb is buffer-only, never a competing durable writer
work_items.decision: The transient-buffer-vs-durable contradiction is resolved by binding this row to the R1 verified cutover; redb's transient-buffer role is realized only as the durable class moves to PostgreSQL under parity, not by assumption.
work_items.source: STORE-001 decision_summary + correction (transitional durable-on-redb); inventory PGREDB-006 (NUPG-CLAIM-007, INGEST-CLAIM-002)

work_items.surface: PGREDB-007 — shared redb state read view (LifeOS UI reads the same redb file agents write, for low-latency swarm-status over UDS or shared redb state; proposed)
work_items.resolved_family: PGAUTH-006-M5 (projection / read-view) over PGAUTH-006-M3 (transient agent state)
work_items.owner: agents write the underlying state; LifeOS UI is a read-only projection (multi-reader)
work_items.overlap_resolved: Prevents a second writable authority: the UI view is read-only; the interactive control path may instead be UDS with redb-backed views
work_items.sync_edge: read-only projection: canonical agent state -> UI view (UDS or shared read); no write path from the UI
work_items.idempotency: view is disposable and re-derivable; reads are idempotent
work_items.identity_boundary: shared file path + table identity (read scope only)
work_items.recovery_source: re-read from the canonical agent state; the view holds no authority
work_items.forbidden_dual_write: must not become a dual-writer over agent state (source itself flagged the overlap risk)
work_items.decision: Shared redb state is a read-only UI projection over the transient agent tier; never a second writable authority.
work_items.source: inventory PGREDB-007 (BAREMETAL-CLAIM-005, SWARM-CLAIM-009/012); blueprint LifeOS UDS / shared-redb-read section; PGAUTH-004 projection territory

matrix_invariants: Every recorded state family (5 pgauth_closure_matrix families + 7 PGREDB inventory rows, mapped onto STORE-001's 6 canonical classes) is assigned exactly once.
Single durable writer: PostgreSQL + RuVector owns every durable macro-state class exactly once; redb never becomes a second durable writer for a PostgreSQL-owned class.
Every synchronization edge is directional (forward-only) and idempotent (SHA-256 / BLAKE3 / SHAKE256 / sequence+idempotency-key / deterministic re-projection).
Every family names an identity boundary and a recovery source.
No unresolved dual-write path remains; the CONTESTED PGREDB-006 row is bound to the R1 verified cutover rather than left open.
Verified cutover: a class leaves redb-durable only after a green live blobstore_parity.rs run against a disposable PostgreSQL; never by assumption (STORE-001 invariant).
No product execution: all downstream execution targets (POSTGRES-002 and product-implementation tasks) remain Draft while the global graph gate is closed.
completion_gate: The matrix covers every recorded state family exactly once, resolves all overlaps and gaps, makes each synchronization edge directional and idempotent, names identity and recovery authorities, contains no unresolved dual-write path, and keeps every execution target Draft while the global graph gate is closed.
